app.title: 8D Report Generator
app.translatingReport: Translating report to the selected language...

info.heroDesc: Structured problem-solving per IATF 16949 — no Excel chaos, no template hunting. Fill in the fields, AI generates, download PDF and Excel.
info.outputPdfDesc: Professionally formatted and audit-ready, with company logo. Contains all eight disciplines across structured pages. Ready to forward to your OEM or auditor immediately.
info.outputExcelDesc: Fully editable Excel file for internal follow-up. All fields structured and compatible with common QM systems. Ideal for ongoing documentation and tracking.
info.disciplinesSub: The generator guides you through every discipline and ensures no mandatory field is missed — exactly as auditors and OEMs require.
info.d1Desc: Team composition, responsibilities and team leader
info.d2Desc: Structured failure description using the 5W method
info.d3Desc: Immediate containment actions to limit damage
info.d4Desc: 5-Why analysis in three blocks: occurrence cause, non-detection cause, systemic cause
info.d5Desc: Selection and verification of permanent corrective measures
info.d7Desc: Transfer of learnings to similar processes and products
info.d8Desc: Acknowledgement of team contributions and formal report closure
info.howTitle: Three steps to a finished report
info.step1Desc: Purchase a credit and enter your access code. No registration, no user account required.
info.step2Desc: Complete the guided input fields — from problem description to root cause analysis. The wizard leads you step by step.
info.step3Desc: AI generates your complete 8D report as PDF and Excel. Audit-ready and shareable immediately.
info.pricingNote: All prices excl. 19% VAT · Invoice sent automatically by email · Secure payment via Stripe
info.sampleTitle: See the result before you buy
info.sampleDesc: Download a real demo report and check for yourself whether the format meets your audit and OEM requirements.
info.faq1Q: Do I need to create an account?
info.faq1A: No. Access works entirely via the access code you receive by email after purchase. No registration, no password required.
info.faq2Q: Which languages is the report generated in?
info.faq2A: German and English. You choose the output language when filling in the form. Both outputs are complete and standard-compliant.
info.faq3A: Typically under 60 seconds after completing your inputs. PDF and Excel are immediately available for download.
info.faq4A: No. Credits purchased are permanently valid and can be redeemed at any time.
info.faq5A: All data is processed and stored on EU servers in compliance with GDPR. Input data is not stored permanently after the report is generated.
info.finalCtaDesc: Choose your credit package and generate your first audit-ready 8D report — today.

unlock.heading: 8D Generator
unlock.subtitle: Enter your access code to continue
unlock.label: Access Code
unlock.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

s1.metaDesc: Basic details about the 8D report. Fields marked with * are required.
s1.templateFlow: Template flow: D0 report identification followed by D1 team formation.
s1.idSectionDesc: Capture the header information in the same order used by the Excel template.
s1.contextSectionDesc: Group customer, product, supplier, complaint, and lot information as shown in the template.
s1.controlSectionDesc: Track urgency, ownership, department, and report status before the investigation begins.
s1.symptomSectionDesc: Summarize the observed symptom so the report header aligns with the export layout.
s1.customerPartNumberPh: Part number used by the customer
s1.d1Desc: Define the cross-functional team responsible for this 8D report.
s1.teamCoreDesc: List the main owners shown in the D1 section of the template.
s1.teamSupportDesc: Add management support and any extra team members or departments involved.
s1.additionalMembersPh: Comma-separated names or one per line

s2.title: D2 — Problem Description
s2.desc: Describe the problem in detail. This information will be used by AI to generate root-cause analysis and corrective actions. Fields marked with * are required.
s2.templateFlow: Template flow: complaint text, symptom breakdown, IS / IS NOT, then internal coding.
s2.complaintSectionDesc: Start D2 with the original complaint text to match the Excel section order.
s2.symptomSectionDesc: Document what, where, when, quantity, and detection details in the same order used for export.
s2.whyProblemSectionDesc: Explain why the described error is a problem from the customer's perspective. This is a key question for the 8D analysis.
s2.extendedSectionDesc: Capture added detail for deviation, impact, occurrence pattern, and working notes.
s2.whatPh: Describe the defect or nonconformity in detail...
s2.howPh: Describe the sequence or pattern of occurrence
s2.whyProblem: Why is this a problem for the customer? *
s2.whyProblemPh: Explain why this error is a problem from the customer's perspective (e.g. impact on form, fit, or function)
s2.quantitativeDeviationPh: e.g. 2.5 mm oversize, 14% scrap, 3 lots affected
s2.quantitativeHint: Enter measurable deviations here (dimensions, defect rates, quantities).
s2.qualitativeDescriptionPh: Describe the defect appearance or behavior
s2.qualitativeHint: Enter qualitative deviations here (visual defects, surface quality, color deviations).
s2.customerImpactPh: Explain the effect on the customer or next process
s2.isIsNotDesc: Describe what the problem IS and what it IS NOT for each dimension.
s2.codeSectionDesc: Keep the internal failure code close to the structured D2 analysis, as in the template.

s3.title: D3 — Containment Actions
s3.desc: Define immediate containment actions to protect the customer. At least one action with a responsible person is required.
s3.templateFlow: Template flow: action table first, containment verification second.
s3.actionPlanDesc: Lay out each containment action like the Excel action table, including scope and risk coverage.
s3.empty: No containment actions yet. Click the button below to add one.
s3.actionPh: What immediate action will be taken?
s3.riskAssessmentPh: Residual risk, severity, or exposure summary
s3.verificationDesc: Record how the short-term actions were checked for effectiveness before moving forward.
s3.deliveryTraceabilityDesc: Capture the shipment and quantity traceability linked to the containment delivery.
s3.effectivenessVerificationPh: Describe how containment effectiveness was verified

s4.aiDesc: Click the button below to generate root cause analysis and corrective action suggestions based on the problem description you entered in Step 2. You can edit all AI-generated content.
s4.templateFlow: Template flow: TUA, TUN, systemic causes, then corrective action planning.
s4.d4Desc: Two 5-Why chains: one for how the defect occurred, one for how it escaped detection. Edit as needed.
s4.occurrenceDesc: Follow the same sequence as the Excel TUA section: possible cause, why chain, root cause, and coding.
s4.detectionDesc: Capture why the defect was not detected before shipment or the next process.
s4.tuaDesc: Why did the defect occur in the process or product?
s4.tunDesc: Why did the controls fail to detect the defect?
s4.suaDesc: What in the management system allowed the occurrence cause to exist?
s4.sunDesc: What in the management system allowed the detection gap to remain?
s4.systemicSectionDesc: Mirror the Excel systemic block by documenting cause text, coding, and derivation for SUA and SUN.
s4.causeCodePh: Optional code from your internal cause catalog
s4.possibleCausePh: Initial suspected cause before the full why chain is confirmed
s4.causeDomainPh: e.g. Man, Machine, Method, Material, Measurement
s4.d5Desc: Actions to eliminate the root causes identified above. Assign responsibilities and target dates.
s4.actionPlanningDesc: Structure each corrective action like the Excel action plan, with cause linkage and ownership.
s4.linkedCauseCodePh: Optional code to trace the action to a cause
s4.verificationPh: How will this action be verified?
s4.verificationPlanningDesc: Capture the method, KPI, and review approach for confirming action effectiveness.

s5.d6Desc: Track the implementation status of corrective actions and verification results.
s5.templateFlow: Template flow: D6 execution, D7 document prevention, then D8 closure.
s5.d6ExecutionDesc: Match the D6 header row by capturing status, implementation date, and responsible owner together.
s5.d6VerificationDesc: Document verification results and when containment can be safely removed.
s5.d7Desc: Systemic actions to prevent similar problems from occurring.
s5.d7DocsDesc: Mirror the prevention matrix from the Excel template across each controlled document type.
s5.d8Desc: Finalize the report with customer approval, lessons learned, and team recognition.
s5.d8ApprovalDesc: Keep the formal closure and customer sign-off fields grouped together like the final template block.
s5.d8LearningDesc: Close the report with organizational learning and acknowledgement of the team contribution.
s5.teamRecognitionPh: Acknowledge the team's effort and contributions...
s5.generateD6D7Desc: Based on your D5 corrective actions, AI can generate implementation verification (D6) and systemic prevention measures (D7).
s5.aiError: AI generation failed. Please try again.

preview.title: Report Preview
preview.desc: Review all sections before exporting. Use the Back button to make changes.
preview.confirmExportDesc: Are you sure you want to proceed to export? Your input data will not be saved after this step.

export.title: Export Report
export.desc: Download your 8D report in PDF or Excel format.
export.pdfDesc: Single formatted document containing all 8D sections. Suitable for customer submission and archiving.
export.xlsxDesc: Multi-sheet workbook with structured data across 6 sheets. Suitable for data analysis and internal tracking.
export.languageDialogDesc: Select the language for this exported report. AI translation is only used for the download copy when needed.

ai.failedMsg: AI generation failed. You can edit the fields manually.

sufficiency.pass: Data is sufficient for AI generation.

validation.futureDate: The date must be in the future.
